title: EV Charging Intelligence: Price Forecasting & Market Optimization
subtitle: 24-Hour Ahead Electricity Price Forecasting + Nash Equilibrium Pricing for the I-210 Corridor
author: Nakul Sreekanth
domain: Energy Markets / EV Infrastructure / Sustainable Transportation

modules.name: Forecasting
modules.description: 24-hour ahead real-time electricity price forecasting for CAISO SP15 zone
modules.award: SAS Global Forum Curiosity Cup 2026 Finalist

modules.name: Optimization
modules.description: Nash equilibrium pricing model for 51 EV charging stations on I-210 corridor
modules.model: Logit discrete-choice demand + iterative best-response via PROC OPTMODEL

key_findings: LSTM achieves test MAE of ~$10.99/MWh and R²≈0.48 on 5,567 out-of-sample forecasts
Both LSTM and ARMA struggle with 17-21h ramp hours — a genuine information limit, not a modeling failure
Nash equilibrium prices remain stable despite peak demand surges — competition prevents opportunistic pricing
Stations exceed 200-300% of rated capacity during afternoon/evening — physical constraints dominate, not pricing power
Profits are evenly distributed across all 51 stations, confirming near-competitive market structure
Expanding capacity is more effective than adjusting prices for reducing congestion on the corridor
